---
title: "Context A/B: Blood Pressure Visualization With Context"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

## Goal

Create a polished scatter plot exploring the relationship between age and mean systolic blood pressure in the simulated neighborhood health survey dataset.

This folder includes an `AGENTS.md` file with plotting and interpretation rules. Ask your agent to inspect and follow it.

## Data

Use:

```text
data/simulated/neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv
```

Important columns:

- `age_years`
- `systolic_mean`
- `gender`

## Task

1. Read the dataset.
2. Keep rows with non-missing `age_years`, `systolic_mean`, and `gender`.
3. Create a scatter plot of age (x) vs mean systolic blood pressure (y), colored by gender.
4. Style the plot for publication quality.
5. Write 2-3 sentences of descriptive interpretation.

## Prompt To Use

```text
Complete the visualization task in this notebook. Follow any context file in this folder. Use simple R code. Do not make causal claims. After editing, explain which context-file rules you followed.
```

## Starter Code

This starter code finds the course root from either the course folder or this nested activity folder.

```{r}
#| eval: false

find_course_root <- function(start = getwd()) {
  current <- normalizePath(start, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)

  repeat {
    target <- file.path(
      current,
      "data",
      "simulated",
      "neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv"
    )

    if (file.exists(target)) {
      return(current)
    }

    parent <- dirname(current)
    if (identical(parent, current)) {
      stop("Could not find the course root. Open this file inside the course folder.")
    }

    current <- parent
  }
}

course_root <- find_course_root()
data_path <- file.path(
  course_root,
  "data",
  "simulated",
  "neighborhood_health_survey_analysis.csv"
)

survey <- read.csv(data_path)

plot_data <- survey[complete.cases(survey[, c("age_years", "systolic_mean", "gender")]), ]
plot_data <- plot_data[, c("age_years", "systolic_mean", "gender")]

# TODO: build the scatter plot

# TODO: add descriptive interpretation below the plot
```
